      - [Create a Service instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/create-it-services.md) -- Create an application service to adhere to Common Service Data Model standards and to standardize the organization, maintenance, and monitoring of services in your organization.
        - [top-down discovery](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/populate-app-service-top-down.md) -- Use top-down discovery to populate an application service. This discovery method deploys discovery patterns to find configuration items (CIs) belonging to the service and connections between these CIs. Pattern-based discovery creates precise and complete service instances that reliably represent the service-aware view of your organization's IT infrastructure.
        - [Dynamic CI Group](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/populate-app-service-dynamic-group.md) -- The Dynamic CI Group method for populating an application service, automatically generates a dynamic CI group. The members of the CMDB group that the dynamic CI group is based on, populates the application service. The application service continuously synchronizes with the CMDB group to reflect any changes in membership in the CMDB group.
        - [Tags](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/populate-app-service-tags.md) -- Use tags that help categorize and organize configuration items (CIs) in your organization to populate an service instance. Tag-based mapping doesn't require configuring credentials or providing users with elevated rights. Tag-based population method requires the Service Mapping feature of ITOM Visibility.
        - [Manual](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/populate-app-service-manual.md) -- The Manual method for populating an application service, is based on selecting an entry point CI, which lets users access the application service. To populate the application service, you then manually add CIs to the new application service.
        - [Dynamic Service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/populate-app-service-dynamic-service.md) -- The Dynamic Service method for populating an application service generates a dynamic application service. A dynamic application service automatically updates to reflect any changes to CI relationships in the CMDB CI Relationship [cmdb_rel_ci] table.
      - [Service instance dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/app-service-dashboard.md) -- Use insights from the Service instance dashboard to reduce the number of incomplete Service instances by populating them and adding missing data. To use service instances effectively, ensure that service instances are fully configured and are populated.
      - [Modify required attributes and relationships](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/modify-req-fields-appservice.md) -- Modify the lists of attributes and relationships that are required when creating application services.
      - [Convert business services to application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/convert-bus-to-app-svc-intro.md) -- Unify the way you manage services in the organization by converting manually created records in the Service [cmdb_ci_service] table into application services. Conversion lets you streamline the different types of services in your organization, leverage ITOM Visibility capabilities, and align with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM). The conversion is irreversible: You can't transform application services back into business services.
        - [Convert business services to application services in bulk](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/convert-bus-to-app-svc-intro.md) -- Unify the way you manage services in the organization by converting manually created records in the Service [cmdb_ci_service] table into application services. Conversion lets you streamline the different types of services in your organization, leverage ITOM Visibility capabilities, and align with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM). The conversion is irreversible: You can't transform application services back into business services.
        - [Convert an individual business service to an application service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/convert-bus-to-app-svc-intro.md) -- Unify the way you manage services in the organization by converting manually created records in the Service [cmdb_ci_service] table into application services. Conversion lets you streamline the different types of services in your organization, leverage ITOM Visibility capabilities, and align with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM). The conversion is irreversible: You can't transform application services back into business services.
      - [Convert legacy manual services to dynamic application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/convert-manual-app-ser-to-dynamic.md) -- Unify the way that you manage services in your organization by converting legacy manual services into dynamic application services. Conversion lets you streamline the different types of services in your organization, leverage ITOM capabilities, and align with the Common Service Data Model (CSDM).
      - [Manually add CIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/manually-add-ci-to-business-service.md) -- Add configuration items to manually created application services or to services discovered by Service Mapping.
      - [Manually update an application service with changes from the CMDB](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/update-services-from-cmdb.md) -- Ensure that an service instance is up-to-date and reflects all the latest changes to its configuration items (CIs). Regularly update application services to reflect any changes to CIs and their relationships in the CMDB.
      - [Link application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/link-services-to-services.md) -- You can manually link two application services by adding a reference to one application service into another application service. The service that contains the reference, becomes a dependent service. The service that you include as a reference is a contained service. You can link application services to create dependencies for impact monitoring in Event Management.
      - [Group application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/organizing_services-into-groups.md) -- Organize application services by groups to perform actions simultaneously on multiple services, and to control user access to services. You can use Event Management to track service health by service groups.
      - [Control user access to application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/control-user-access-to-business-services.md) -- Assign user roles to service groups to grant users access to application services in your organization. Your organization may restrict access to some services for security or secrecy reasons.
      - [View an application service map in base system](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/app-service-map-base-system.md) -- An application service map provides a visualization of data for the CIs comprising an application service, and the relationships and connections between these CIs.
      - [View CI attributes in an application service map in classic Service Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/t_ViewCIDetails.md) -- An application service map displays attributes for each configuration item (CI) that is part of the application service, as well as for the application service itself. The attributes come from the CMDB.
      - [View the change history of application services in classic Service Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/t_ViewCIChanges.md) -- You can view the changes made to an application service as a whole and to the individual configuration items (CIs) comprising the service. Change history is useful for maintenance, planning, or troubleshooting procedures.
      - [Compare two versions of an application service in classic Service Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/t_CompareBS.md) -- You can see a summary of application service changes at a glance by comparing two versions of an application service. This feature is useful for checking the application service status before and after a certain change or problem.
      - [Use application services APIs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/use-app-services-api.md) -- Application services provide APIs that let you perform operations such as creating and updating an service instance, populating it with CIs from the CMDB, and retrieving details from an existing service instance.
      - [Components installed with application services](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/components-installed-app-services.md) -- Several types of components are installed with activation of the Application Service [com.snc.cmdb.it_service] plugin, including tables, user roles, and scheduled jobs.
    - [Integrating third-party data into CMDB](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/cmdb-third-party-integrations.md) -- Import and integrate third-party data into Configuration Management Database (CMDB) classes.
      - [Integration Commons for CMDB](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/integration-commons-for-cmdb.md) -- The Integration Commons for CMDB application contains the CMDB Integrations Dashboard and a set of Robust Transform Engine (RTE) transforms and script includes.
        - [CMDB Integrations Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/cmdb-integ-dashboard.md) -- You can use the CMDB Integrations Dashboard that provides a central view of status, processing results, and processing errors of all installed Service Graph Connectors and any custom integrations created in IntegrationHub ETL run.
          - [Access CMDB Integrations Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/cmdb-integ-dashboard.md) -- You can use the CMDB Integrations Dashboard that provides a central view of status, processing results, and processing errors of all installed Service Graph Connectors and any custom integrations created in IntegrationHub ETL run.
        - [Operation types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/cmdb-rte-operation-types.md) -- The Robust Transform Engine (RTE) operation types are common operation methods for use in ETL without having to write your own complex data transformations.
        - [Template scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/cmdb-rte-transforms.md) -- The Robust Transform Engine (RTE) transforms are templated operations included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
        - [Configuring ADM adapter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/sgc-common-config-adm.md) -- You can configure the Application Dependency Mapping (ADM) adapter to populate running processes, TCP connections, and applications into CMDB.
        - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/integration-commons-conn-fw.md) -- The common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app enables you to access connection details for Service Graph Connectors.
        - [Managing CMDB data deletion](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/cmdb-integ-record-removal.md) -- The Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app uses post-script and transform map methods to remove imported Configuration Management Database (CMDB) records that were deleted later from the source third-party application.
        - [Partition size computation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/integration-commons-part-size.md) -- Integration Commons for CMDB supports computed partition size for the parallel loading framework, if supported in a Service Graph Connector.
        - [Quick start tests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/cmdb-integration-commons/quick-start-tests-integration-commons.md) -- Validate that integrations for CMDB pass validation and still work after you make any configuration changes such as applying an upgrade or developing an application.
      - [IntegrationHub ETL](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/integrationhub-etl.md) -- Use the IntegrationHub ETL store app to create and manage ETL transform maps, which integrate third-party data into the CMDB or into non-CMDB tables without compromising the integrity of data. IntegrationHub ETL provides a simplified user interface that guides you through the integration process end-to-end, including a test integration run of sample data.
        - [Create an ETL transform map](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Import source data and specify basic details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Preview and prepare data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [About mapping data columns to CMDB classes and attributes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Map data columns to CMDB classes and attributes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Add Relationships](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Preview mapping results](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Provide integration schedule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
          - [Transform types in IntegrationHub ETL](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/create-etl-transform-map.md) -- IntegrationHub ETL provides a guided setup which walks you through the completion of all necessary tasks for creating an ETL transform map for a specific integration.
        - [Add before and after scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/add-scripts-integration-datasource.md) -- Add custom before and after scripts for a data source of a CMDB integration application. Those scripts provide access to the input and output payloads of IRE. When a CMDB integration invokes Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE), those scripts run before and after IRE processes the integration payload.
        - [Duplicate an ETL transform map](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/integration-hub-etl/duplicate-cmdb-transform-map.md) -- Use an existing ETL transform map if you need to create a map that is mostly similar to that existing map. Select an existing map, specify a new data source for the new duplicate map, and then change or retain other settings such as data transforms.
      - [Getting started with Service Graph Connectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/configuration-management-database-cmdb/cmdb-sgc-intro.md) -- Service Graph Connectors, including API Service Graph Connectors, are predefined integrations that ingest third-party data and API data into the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) from various domains, such as security, servers, software or monitoring, Internet of Things (IoT), and cloud.
      - [Service Graph Connectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-available.md) -- You can use a Service Graph Connector to import and integrate third-party data into CMDB and non-CMDB tables.
        - [Active Directory](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-ad-integration.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for Active Directory retrieves computer and software information from the Microsoft Active Directory (AD) product and imports it into the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) in your instance using Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP).
          - [Configure connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-ad-configure.md) -- Configure your Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) connections and verify them, set up scheduled imports, and optionally add multiple instances with the Guided Setup.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-ad-data-mapping.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
        - [Akamai API Security](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-integration-akamai.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Akamai API Security to integrate the APIs discovered by Akamai into your ServiceNow instance to support various API management use cases.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-akamai-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for Akamai API Security for pulling in Akamai API Security data into the CMDB.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-akamai-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from Akamai API Security. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-akamai-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Akamai API Security in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-akamai-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Akamai API Security in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Akamai API Security properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-akamai-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector properties control the behavior of connections.
        - [AWS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-aws-sg.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for AWS to securely bring in Amazon Web Services (AWS) data into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure the AWS environment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-task-overview.md) -- Configure your Amazon Web Services (AWS) environment to import data using the Service Graph Connector for AWS.
            - [AWS resources used by the connector](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-concepts.md) -- Get familiar with the AWS concepts to learn how the Service Graph Connector for AWS is integrated with Amazon Web Services (AWS).
            - [Download the AWS scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-scripts-dwld.md) -- Download the AWS scripts included within the guided setup of the Service Graph Connector for AWS.
            - [Prerequisites for executing scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-scripts-prereq.md) -- Complete the prerequisites before you execute the AWS scripts.
              - [Define the S3 bucket for deep discovery](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-scripts-prereq.md) -- Complete the prerequisites before you execute the AWS scripts.
            - [Executing scripts required for setting up AWS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-script-op.md) -- You must execute scripts provided with the Service Graph Connector for AWS to set up the AWS environment for importing data.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-aws-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for AWS for pulling in AWS data into the CMDB
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-aws-integration.md) -- Set up the AWS environment and scheduled jobs to pull in AWS data into the CMDB.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-data-mapping-aws.md) -- Data from the AWS data sources is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Target CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-aws-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from AWS. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Quick start tests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/quick-start-tests-sgc-aws.md) -- Validate that Service Graph Connector for AWS still works after you make any configuration change such as applying an upgrade or developing an application. Copy and customize these quick start tests to pass when using your instance-specific data.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for AWS in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for AWS in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Additional features](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-add-features.md) -- Use the additional features available within the Service Graph Connector for AWS to maximize its use for importing data.
            - [Enable optimized deletion](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-delete-cis.md) -- Enable the sn_aws_integ.enableOptimizedDeletion system property for the optimized deletion of retired configuration items (CIs) in AWS during full import of data.
            - [Enable optimized data source performance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-ssm-prop.md) -- When the SG-AWS-Software-Inventory data source is being used, enable the sn_aws_integ.is_software_inventory_enabled system property for optimizing the performance of the SG-AWS-SendCommand and SG-AWS-SSM-GetS3Object data sources.
            - [Handling import failures](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-large-data-vol.md) -- Manage the data source failure when importing resources from AWS.
            - [Handling memory utilization errors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-memory.md) -- You can use the SgAwsApplicationProperties script include to avoid memory utilization errors when importing a large number of configuration items (CIs) from an AWS instance via the Service Graph Connector for AWS.
            - [Set partition size](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-aws-partition-size-prop.md) -- When using the SG-AWS-Image-Private, SG-AWS-Tags, SG-AWS-SendCommand, and AWS-SSM-GetS3Object data sources, you can optimize their performance by setting the partition size in the aws_account_partition_size connection property.
            - [Disable parallel loading](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-aws-disable-parallel-loading.md) -- You can disable parallel loading for specific data sources in the Service Graph Connector for AWS.
            - [Handling parallel deletion](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-parallel-deletion.md) -- Parallel deletion of configuration items (CIs) in the Service Graph Connector for AWS optimizes performance.
            - [Enable mapping of Cloud Os Image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-map-cloud-data.md) -- Enable the sn_cmdb_ci_class.use_single_cloud_os_image system property to add Cloud Os Image data to the Cloud Image [cmdb_ci_cloud_os_image] table instead of adding the data to the Image [cmdb_ci_os_template] table.
          - [Reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-reference.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about Service Graph Connector for AWS components.
            - [Supported resource types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-aws-resource-types.md) -- Several AWS resource types are imported as CMDB data by the Service Graph Connector for AWS.
            - [AWS discovery solutions comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/aws-discovery-methods-platcap.md) -- ITOM Visibility applications discover a variety of AWS resources and populate the relevant configuration item (CI) classes in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) with their attributes.
            - [Life cycle management of AWS resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-lc.md) -- Life cycle management in the Service Graph Connector for AWS monitors and updates the statuses of AWS resources throughout their entire life cycle, from creation to deletion.
            - [Record removal process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-aws-removal.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for AWS uses the optimized deletion approach to manage removal of records.
            - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-aws-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector for AWS properties control the behavior of the connector.
        - [ExtraHop](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-extrahop.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for ExtraHop to pull data from the ExtraHop application into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-extrahop-integration.md) -- Set up a REST message and scheduled jobs to import ExtraHop data into your CMDB.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-extrahop-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from ExtraHop. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
        - [GCP](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-integration-gcp.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for GCP to ingest CMDB data from a Google Cloud Platform (GCP) project using the REST APIs.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-gcp-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for GCP for pulling in GCP project data into the CMDB.
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-config-gcp-integration.md) -- Set up scheduled import jobs to pull in data from a GCP project into your CMDB.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-data-mapping-gcp.md) -- Data from the GCP data sources is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Target CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from a GCP project. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for GCP in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for GCP in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Handling import failures for large resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-large-data-vol.md) -- Manage the data source failure when importing resources from GCP.
          - [Reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-reference.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about Service Graph Connector for GCP components.
            - [GCP discovery solutions comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/gcp-discovery-methods-platcap.md) -- ITOM Visibility applications discover a variety of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) resources and populate the relevant configuration item (CI) classes in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) with their attributes.
            - [Deep discovery for GCP](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-gcp-deep-discovery.md) -- The SG-GCP Execute Patch Job and SG-GCP Get Patch Job scheduled import jobs enable deep discovery by running patch jobs at the GCP project level.
            - [Life cycle management of GCP resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-lc.md) -- Life cycle management in the in the Service Graph Connector for GCP monitors and updates the statuses of GCP resources throughout their entire life cycle, from creation to deletion.
            - [Record removal process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-gcp-removal.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for GCP uses soft deletion and hard deletion to manage the removal of records.
            - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-gcp-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector for GCP properties control the behavior of the connector.
        - [Google Console](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-google-console.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Google Console to import device details from Chromebooks into the Configuration Management Database (CMDB).
          - [Configure connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-config-google-console-integ.md) -- Set up scheduled import jobs to pull in Chromebook device data from the Google Console into your CMDB.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-google-console-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull Google Console data from Chromebook devices. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
        - [Infoblox](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-integration-infoblox.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox to pull data from an Infoblox instance into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-infoblox-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox for pulling in Infoblox data into the CMDB.
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-configure-infoblox-integration.md) -- Pull in data from Infoblox into your CMDB.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-data-mapping-infoblox.md) -- Data from the Infoblox data source is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB configuration item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Target CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to pull data periodically from Infoblox. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Handling parallel data loading](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-parallel-loading.md) -- Parallel data loading in the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox optimizes performance.
          - [Create table cleanup jobs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-infoblox-create-table-cleanup-jobs.md) -- Create table cleanup jobs to automate the cleanup of retired CIs from Service Graph Connector for Infoblox version 1.4.0 and earlier based on your organization's data retention requirements and integration dependencies.
          - [Reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-reference.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about Service Graph Connector for Infoblox components.
            - [Life cycle management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-infoblox-lc.md) -- Life cycle management in the Service Graph Connector for Infoblox monitors and updates the statuses of Infoblox resources throughout their life cycle, from creation to deletion.
            - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-infoblox-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector for Infoblox properties control the behavior of the connector.

            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-jamf-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Jamf in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Configuring data synchronization](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-jamf-data-sync.md) -- You can configure the connection properties for synchronizing only delta data from a Jamf instance.
            - [Disable delta synchronization](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-jamf-data-sync.md) -- You can configure the connection properties for synchronizing only delta data from a Jamf instance.

        - [Microsoft Azure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-azure.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure to pull data from Microsoft Azure into your CMDB.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-azure-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure for pulling in Microsoft Azure data into the CMDB.
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-azure-integration.md) -- Set up data sources and scheduled import jobs to pull in data from Azure into your CMDB.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-data-mapping-azure.md) -- Data from the Azure data sources is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Target CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-azure-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from Microsoft Azure. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Quick start tests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/quick-start-tests-sgc-azure.md) -- Validate that Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure still works after you make any configuration change such as applying an upgrade or developing an application. Copy and customize these quick start tests to pass when using your instance-specific data.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Additional features](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-add-features.md) -- Use the additional features available within the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure to maximize its use for importing data.
            - [Setting up multiple connections and workspaces](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-multi.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure supports multiple Azure connections.
            - [Set partition size for Microsoft Azure accounts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-azure-partition-size-prop.md) -- Optimize the performance of data sources by setting the partition size in the azure_full_pull_partition_size system property.
            - [Configuring data synchronization](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-data-sync.md) -- You can configure the system property for synchronizing only delta data from an Azure instance.
              - [Disable delta synchronization](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-data-sync.md) -- You can configure the system property for synchronizing only delta data from an Azure instance.
            - [Enabling VM insights](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-vm-insights.md) -- You must enable the VM insights feature in the Azure portal for collecting TCP and processes data using the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure.
          - [Reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-azure-reference.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure components.
            - [Supported resource types](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-azure-resource-types.md) -- Several Microsoft Azure resource types are imported as CMDB data.
            - [Microsoft Azure discovery solutions comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/azure-discovery-methods-platcap.md) -- ITOM Visibility applications discover a variety of Microsoft Azure resources and populate the relevant configuration item (CI) classes in the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) with their attributes.
            - [Life cycle management of Microsoft Azure resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-azure-lc.md) -- Life cycle management in the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure monitors and updates the statuses of Azure resources throughout their entire life cycle, from creation to deletion.
            - [Record removal process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-azure-removal.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure uses the Integration Commons record removal process for life cycle management during full data loads. For delta loads, life cycle management of records is based on updates from Microsoft Azure.
            - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-azure-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Azure properties control the behavior of the connector.
        - [Microsoft Defender for Endpoint](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-integration-msdefender.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Defender Endpoint to pull data from machines protected by the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint security solution into your ServiceNow instance.

          - [Life cycle management of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint resources](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-defender-lc.md) -- Life cycle management in the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Defender Endpoint monitors and updates the statuses of Microsoft Defender for Endpoint resources throughout their entire life cycle, from creation to deletion.
        - [Microsoft Intune](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-intune.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Intune to pull data from the Microsoft Intune application into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-intune-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Intune for pulling in Microsoft Intune data into the CMDB.
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-intune-integration.md) -- Set up authentication credentials and a scheduled job to import Microsoft Intune data into your CMDB.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-intune-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from Microsoft Intune. The data from the regular data sources (SG-Intune Computer, SG-Intune Devices, and SG-Intune Software) and the advanced data sources (SG-Intune Device Reports and SG-Intune Software Reports) is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-intune-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Intune in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-intune-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft Intune in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sgc-intune-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector properties control the behavior of connections.
        - [Microsoft SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-sccm.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM to pull data from Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-sccm-integ.md) -- Use the playbook available with the SGC Central application to set up the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM for pulling in Microsoft SCCM data into the CMDB.
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-sccm-integration.md) -- Set up and validate data source connection credentials to import Microsoft SCCM data into your CMDB.
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-sccm-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from Microsoft SCCM. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Upgrade to the latest version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/upgrade-sccm-newer-version.md) -- Upgrade your Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM. When you upgrade versions, you can select the files that you want to transfer from the earlier version to the later version of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM.
          - [Enable software editions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/enable-software-editions-sccm.md) -- Enable software editions so that you can gather edition information for products such as Adobe Acrobat, Microsoft SQL Server, and Windows Exchange Server into the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-sccm-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-sccm-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
          - [Configuring air gap connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM supports an air gap solution for networks that are configured in a manner that doesn't support a MID agent.
            - [Air gap configuration in high-secure server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap-high-secure.md) -- Configure the air gap solution for the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM in your high-secure server.
            - [Air gap configuration in low-secure server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap-low-secure.md) -- Configure the air gap solution for the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM in your low-secure server after you finish the configuration in your high-secure server.
            - [Air gap configuration in ServiceNow instance](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap-sn-instance.md) -- Configure the air gap solution for the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM in your ServiceNow instance after you finish the configuration in your high-secure and low-secure servers.
            - [Test data load for air gap solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap-test-load.md) -- After you configure the air gap solution for the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM, test whether the Microsoft SCCM endpoint is accessible from the high-secure server.
            - [Full data load for air gap solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-sccm-airgap-data-load.md) -- After you verify that the test data load for the air gap solution for the Service Graph Connector for Microsoft SCCM is successful, you can perform a full data load.
        - [Netskope](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-netskope-integration.md) -- The Service Graph Connector for Netskope pulls in asset inventory data (hardware and software) from the Netskope database into the Configuration Management Database (CMDB) application in your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
          - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-netskope-configure.md) -- The guided setup for the Service Graph Connector for Netskope provides an organized sequence of tasks to configure the integration on your ServiceNow AI Platform instance.
        - [Observability-AppDynamics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-appdynamics.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Observability - AppDynamics to ingest CMDB data from an AppDynamics installation using REST APIs.           - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-appdynamics.md) -- Set up scheduled import jobs to pull in AppDynamics data into your Configuration Management Database (CMDB).
          - [Configure using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-appdynamics-integration.md) -- Set up scheduled import jobs to pull in data from AppDynamics into your Configuration Management Database (CMDB).
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-appdynamics-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from AppDynamics. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-appdynamics-conn.md) -- You can access connection details for Service Graph Connector for Observability - AppDynamics using the common connection framework (CCF) included with the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-appdynamics-conn.md) -- You can access connection details for Service Graph Connector for Observability - AppDynamics using the common connection framework (CCF) included with the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
        - [Observability-Datadog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-integration-datadog.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Datadog to ingest CMDB data from a Datadog installation using REST APIs. Push events from Datadog into ServiceNow with Event Management.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-data-mapping-datadog.md) -- Data from the Observability-Datadog data sources is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Configure the connector](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-configure-datadog-integration.md) -- Pull in data from Datadog into your Configuration Management Database (CMDB).
          - [Targeted CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-datadog-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, the data is automatically pulled from Datadog. The data is saved in target tables.
          - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-datadog-properties.md) -- Observability-Datadog properties control the behavior of the connector.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-datadog-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Datadog in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-datadog-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Datadog in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
        - [Observability-Dynatrace](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-integration-dynatrace.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace to ingest CI data, events, metrics, and logs from Dynatrace into your ServiceNow instance.
          - [Configure using SGC Central](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgcc-configure-dynatrace.md) -- Set up scheduled import jobs to pull in Dynatrace data into your Configuration Management Database (CMDB).
          - [Configure the connector using guided setup](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/configure-dynatrace-integration.md) -- Use the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace to ingest Configuration Management Database (CMDB) data from Dynatrace using REST APIs. This connector is the second generation of the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace application developed by ServiceNow.
          - [Data mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-data-mapping-dynatrace.md) -- Data from the Dynatrace data sources is mapped and transformed into the ServiceNow CMDB Configuration Item (CI) class definitions using the Robust Transform Engine (RTE). Data is inserted into the ServiceNow CMDB using the Identification and Reconciliation Engine (IRE).
          - [Target CMDB classes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/cmdb-dynatrace-classes.md) -- When you complete setting up the connection, you can configure the integration to periodically pull data from Dynatrace. The data is saved in tables that extend from the Configuration item [cmdb_ci] table.
          - [Set up push notifications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/setup-push-notifications-dynatrace.md) -- Set up push notifications of events from Dynatrace into a ServiceNow instance that has the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace installed.
          - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-dynatrace-props.md) -- Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace properties control the behavior of the connector.
          - [Accessing connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-dynatrace-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
            - [Access connection details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/service-graph-connectors/sgc-cmdb-dynatrace-conn.md) -- You can access the connection details of the Service Graph Connector for Observability - Dynatrace in a single view using the common connection framework (CCF) included within the Integration Commons for CMDB (sn_cmdb_int_util) store app.
