          - [Workflow update set migration use case - subflow dependency (risk)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowMovementWithUpdateSets.md) -- The system tracks workflows in update sets differently than other records because workflow information is stored across multiple tables.
        - [Input variable movement](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_InputVariableMovement.md) -- You can add input variables to existing workflows and add them to update sets.
          - [Input variable movement use case - two input variables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/r_InptVrblMvmntTwo.md) -- An existing workflow already contains two input variables.
            - [Verify variables included in a specific workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/r_InptVrblMvmntTwo.md) -- An existing workflow already contains two input variables.
          - [Input variable removal](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_InputVariableRemoval.md) -- Deleting workflow input variables, like insert and update actions, creates a customer update record in a user's current update set.
            - [Input variable removal risk](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_InputVariableRemoval.md) -- Deleting workflow input variables, like insert and update actions, creates a customer update record in a user's current update set.
            - [Input variable removal solution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_InputVariableRemoval.md) -- Deleting workflow input variables, like insert and update actions, creates a customer update record in a user's current update set.
            - [Input variable removal prevention](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_InputVariableRemoval.md) -- Deleting workflow input variables, like insert and update actions, creates a customer update record in a user's current update set.
          - [Avoiding duplicate workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_AvoidingDuplicateWorkflows.md) -- Update sets manage the published state of all versions of a workflow prior to committing the workflow version on a local instance.
            - [Commit a workflow in an update set](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_AvoidingDuplicateWorkflows.md) -- Update sets manage the published state of all versions of a workflow prior to committing the workflow version on a local instance.
            - [Update set migration example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_AvoidingDuplicateWorkflows.md) -- Update sets manage the published state of all versions of a workflow prior to committing the workflow version on a local instance.
            - [Update set migration risk](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_AvoidingDuplicateWorkflows.md) -- Update sets manage the published state of all versions of a workflow prior to committing the workflow version on a local instance.
        - [Workflow timelines](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowTimelines.md) -- The system provides a timeline view of history activities associated with a workflow context.
          - [Use a timeline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowTimelines.md) -- The system provides a timeline view of history activities associated with a workflow context.
          - [Timeline for a selected activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowTimelines.md) -- The system provides a timeline view of history activities associated with a workflow context.
          - [View subflows in a workflow timeline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowTimelines.md) -- The system provides a timeline view of history activities associated with a workflow context.
        - [Workflow error handling](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowErrorHandling.md) -- The personalized JavaScript that users create in workflow activity variables is vulnerable to run-time syntax errors. Available error information is available in a tooltip when you point to a workflow activity in an error state.
          - [Workflow error tracking features](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/r_TrackingErrors.md) -- Error handling provides visual cues within the workflow, such as error descriptions for activities in pop-ups, and detailed log records.
          - [Create an error condition exit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/t_CreateAnErrorConditionExit.md) -- An administrator can reroute the workflow when a script error occurs by creating an error condition exit for specific activities within the workflow. This allows the workflow to process script errors in a predictable way and not create undesirable results.
          - [Reconfigure an approval condition](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/t_ReconfigureAnApprovalCondition.md) -- Approval activities react differently to script errors than the other activities.
        - [Workflow run time metrics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowRunTimeMetrics.md) -- You can enable the collection of workflow run time metrics to determine whether workflows are performing as expected or consuming additional resources.
          - [Enable workflow run time metrics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowRunTimeMetrics.md) -- You can enable the collection of workflow run time metrics to determine whether workflows are performing as expected or consuming additional resources.
          - [Outlying workflow run times](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowRunTimeMetrics.md) -- You can enable the collection of workflow run time metrics to determine whether workflows are performing as expected or consuming additional resources.
          - [Workflow estimated run time updates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowRunTimeMetrics.md) -- You can enable the collection of workflow run time metrics to determine whether workflows are performing as expected or consuming additional resources.
        - [Workflow pause utility](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/workflow-pause-utility.md) -- Workflow Pause Utility provides functionality you can use to pause or resume all workflow contexts, a subset of qualified workflow contexts, or individual workflow contexts. You designate the data and time of day at which the paused workflows should resume, and can manually resume individual workflows as needed.
          - [Request workflow pause utility](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/request-workflow-pause-utility.md) -- You can request the Workflow Pause Utility plugin (com.glideapp.workflow.pause) if you have the admin role. This plugin may include demo data and activates related plugins if they are not already active.
          - [Pause and resume individual workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/pause-resume-individual-workflows.md) -- Pause individual workflow contexts. Specify the date and time of day at which the workflow context should resume.
          - [Pause and resume all or multiple workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/pause-resume-all-multiple-workflows.md) -- Use Workflow Pause Group Requests to pause, or resume, groups of workflows, or all active workflows. If pausing a group of workflows, use filtering functions to select the workflows. If pausing all active workflows, select the Pause All check box to indicate that all currently active workflows should be paused.
          - [Monitor workflow pause requests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/monitor-workflow-pause-requests.md) -- You can monitor the status of workflow pause requests using Workflow Pause Request.
        - [Encrypted workflow scratchpad](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/encrypted-workflow-scratchpad.md) -- The Encrypted Workflow Scratchpad plugin (com.snc.encrypted.scratchpad) provides encrypted scratchpad support for workflow context and workflow executing activities.
          - [Request encrypted workflow scratchpad activation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/encrypted-workflow-scratchpad.md) -- The Encrypted Workflow Scratchpad plugin (com.snc.encrypted.scratchpad) provides encrypted scratchpad support for workflow context and workflow executing activities.
      - [Troubleshoot workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_TroubleshootWorkflows.md) -- Troubleshooting tools for workflows enable administrators to isolate execution paths, compare contexts, and track incomplete activities.
        - [Execution path troubleshooting in timelines](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_TroubleshootWorkflows.md) -- Troubleshooting tools for workflows enable administrators to isolate execution paths, compare contexts, and track incomplete activities.
        - [Execution history table](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_TroubleshootWorkflows.md) -- Troubleshooting tools for workflows enable administrators to isolate execution paths, compare contexts, and track incomplete activities.
      - [Use the Workflow Operations Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/t_UseTheWorkflowOpsDashboard.md) -- On the Workflow Operations Dashboard, view and add widgets to help you monitor workflows. Review the performance of workflows and determine which workflows must be improved.
        - [Workflow gauges](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/t_UseTheWorkflowOpsDashboard.md) -- On the Workflow Operations Dashboard, view and add widgets to help you monitor workflows. Review the performance of workflows and determine which workflows must be improved.
      - [Workflow performance timing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowPerformanceTiming.md) -- The workflow engine can generate detailed performance timing data that is useful for troubleshooting slow workflows.
        - [Workflow estimated run time properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowPerformanceTiming.md) -- The workflow engine can generate detailed performance timing data that is useful for troubleshooting slow workflows.
        - [Enable workflow performance timing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/legacy-workflow/c_WorkflowPerformanceTiming.md) -- The workflow engine can generate detailed performance timing data that is useful for troubleshooting slow workflows.
      - [Workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_WorkflowActivities.md) -- Workflow activity properties reference
        - [Workflow activities reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/wf-activity-overview.md) -- Workflow activity reference, organized by category.
          - [Approval and rollback workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_ApprovalAndRollbackActivities.md) -- Approval and rollback activities generate and manage approvals.
            - [Approval Action workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ApprovalAction.md) -- The Approval Action activity performs an approval action on the current task.
            - [Approval Coordinator workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ApprovalCoordinator.md) -- The Approval Coordinator activity creates an approval whose outcome depends on the outcome of one or more child activities, including one or more Approval - User, Approval - Group, and/or Manual Approval activities.
            - [Approval - Group workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ApprovalGroup.md) -- The Approval - Group activity creates approval records for each member of a specified group.
            - [Approval - User workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ApprovalUser.md) -- The Approval - User activity creates one or more individual user approvals.
            - [Generate workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_Generate.md) -- The Generate activity immediately creates task or approval records from any task or approval activities placed after the Generate activity in the workflow path. These pre-generated tasks and approvals start when the task and approval activities are reached during flow execution. This allows a task to have a set of associated pre-generated sequential tasks or approvals, but still require them to be completed in order.
            - [Manual Approvals workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ManualApprovals.md) -- The Manual Approvals activity watches and manages any approvals that users add manually outside of the workflow process. This activity only selects approvals that are in the Not requested state.
            - [Rollback To workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_RollbackTo.md) -- The Rollback To activity transitions directly to the activity specified by the outgoing transition line arrow.
              - [Rollback To behavior](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_RollbackTo.md) -- The Rollback To activity transitions directly to the activity specified by the outgoing transition line arrow.
              - [Rollback To activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_RollbackTo.md) -- The Rollback To activity transitions directly to the activity specified by the outgoing transition line arrow.
          - [Condition Workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ConditionActivites.md) -- Condition activities provide conditional branching and logical operation functionality for workflows.
            - [If workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_If.md) -- The If activity checks a condition or script to determine if a Yes or No transition should be taken.
            - [Switch workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_Switch.md) -- The Switch activity checks if the value of a passed field or variable is equivalent to one of several case values.
            - [Wait for condition workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_WaitForCondition.md) -- The Wait for condition activity causes the workflow to wait at this activity until the current record matches the specified condition.
            - [Wait for WF Event workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_WaitForWFEvent.md) -- The Wait for WF Event activity causes the workflow to wait at this activity until the specified event is fired.
          - [Workflow notification activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_NotificationActivities.md) -- Notification workflow activities notify users of events that occur during the workflow.
            - [Create Event workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_CreateEvent.md) -- The Create Event activity adds an event to the event queue, but does not immediately fire the event.
            - [Notification workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_NotificationActivity.md) -- The Notification activity sends an email or SMS message to specified users or groups.
          - [Timer workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_TimerActivities.md) -- Timer activities pause workflows for set periods of time.
            - [SLA Percentage Timer workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_SLAPercentageTimer.md) -- The SLA Percentage Timer activity pauses the workflow for a duration equal to a percentage of an SLA.
            - [Timer workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_Timer.md) -- The Timer activity pauses the workflow for a specified period of time.
          - [Task workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_TaskActivities.md) -- Task activities create and modify workflow tasks.
            - [Add Worknote workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_AddWorknote.md) -- The Add Worknote activity adds text to the Worknotes field of the current incident record.
            - [Attachment Note workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_AttachmentNote.md) -- The Attachment Note activity adds an attachment to the current record.
            - [Catalog Task workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_CatalogTask.md) -- The Catalog Task activity creates a service catalog task record.
            - [Create Task workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_CreateTask.md) -- The Create Task activity generates a record on any of the tables that extend Task [task].
          - [Utility workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_UtilityActivities.md) -- Utility activities provide controls over the path of the workflow, and other useful tools.
            - [Branch workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_BranchActivity.md) -- The Branch activity splits the workflow into multiple transition paths from a single activity.
            - [Join workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_JoinActivity.md) -- The Join activity unites multiple execution paths into one transition.
            - [Lock workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_LockActivity.md) -- The Lock activity prevents other instances of this workflow from continuing past this activity until the lock is released.
            - [Log Message workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_LogMessageActivity.md) -- The Log Message activity writes a message to the workflow log.
            - [Log Trace Message workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_LogTraceMessage.md) -- The Log Trace Message activity writes a trace message to the workflow log.
            - [REST Message legacy workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_RESTMessageActivity.md) -- The legacy REST Message activity enables an administrator to override the REST endpoint or supply the variables configured in the REST Message module.
            - [Return Value workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ReturnValueActivity.md) -- The Return Value activity returns a value to a parent workflow, when run from a subflow.
            - [Run Script workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_RunScriptActivity.md) -- The Run Script activity runs the specified script in the scope of the workflow version.
            - [Set Values workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_SetValuesActivity.md) -- The Set Values activity sets values on the current record when the workflow quiesces or ends.
            - [SOAP Message legacy workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_SOAPMessageActivity_1.md) -- The legacy SOAP Message activity uses SOAP messages defined in the System Web Services plugin and can call the messages using a MID Server.
            - [Turnstile workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_TurnstileActivity.md) -- The Turnstile activity limits how many times a workflow can pass through the same point.
            - [Unlock workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_UnlockActivity.md) -- The Unlock activity releases a lock that was previously placed by the Lock activity.
          - [Subflow activities in workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_SubflowActivities.md) -- Subflow activities run and manage workflows from a parent workflow.
            - [Parallel Flow Launcher workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ParallelFlowLauncher.md) -- The Parallel Flow Launcher activity launches multiple subflows in parallel.
              - [WorkflowCoordinator object](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_WorkflowCoordinatorObject.md) -- A WorkflowCoordinator object specifies which subflows to run and the input variables to pass to those subflows.
              - [Completed subflow values in scripts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_CompletedSubflowValuesInScripts.md) -- The Parallel Flow Launcher activity exposes additional variables you can use in scripts.
              - [Parallel Flow Launcher example](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/r_ParallelFlowLauncherExample.md) -- This example shows how to use the Parallel Flow Launcheractivity with an array of input values and with a WorkflowCoordinator object.
        - [View workflow activity descriptions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_ViewActivityDescriptions.md) -- Tooltips are available for workflow activities to help you understand how to use each activity.
          - [Elements in workflow activity definitions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_ViewActivityDescriptions.md) -- Tooltips are available for workflow activities to help you understand how to use each activity.
        - [Add an activity to a workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_AddAnActivityToAWorkflow.md) -- Available activities are displayed in the Core, Packs, and Custom tabs in the Workflow Editor palette.
          - [Duplicate a workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_AddAnActivityToAWorkflow.md) -- Available activities are displayed in the Core, Packs, and Custom tabs in the Workflow Editor palette.
        - [Manage transitions between workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_MngTstnsWkflAct.md) -- Transitions define the processing path of the workflow, depending on conditions defined in each activity.
          - [Custom activity transitions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_MngTstnsWkflAct.md) -- Transitions define the processing path of the workflow, depending on conditions defined in each activity.
        - [Manage workflow activity conditions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_ManageActivityConditions.md) -- Activities contain default conditions that determine which transitions are followed.
          - [Activity result value](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_ManageActivityConditions.md) -- Activities contain default conditions that determine which transitions are followed.
        - [Edit the workflow activity properties form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/t_EditActivitiesPropForm.md) -- Customize which workflow variables appear on an activity properties form and how the variables are arranged on the form.
        - [Using workflow approval activities and rolling back workflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/wf-approvals-n-rollbacks.md) -- When you work with approvals, you need to understand how approval activities interact with approval engines, how to correct a skipped approval workflow activity, and how rollbacks work.
          - [Approval workflow activities and approval engines](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/wf-approvals-n-rollbacks.md) -- When you work with approvals, you need to understand how approval activities interact with approval engines, how to correct a skipped approval workflow activity, and how rollbacks work.
          - [Correct a skipped workflow approval activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/wf-approvals-n-rollbacks.md) -- When you work with approvals, you need to understand how approval activities interact with approval engines, how to correct a skipped approval workflow activity, and how rollbacks work.
        - [Using variables in Notify workflow activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_UsingVariablesInNotifyActivities.md) -- Certain Notify workflow activities support variable substitution for reading text to callers.
        - [Use multiple timer activities in one workflow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_MltTmActWkflw.md) -- Workflow timer activities store data independently of each other in an activity-specific scratchpad.
        - [Publish a custom workflow activity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_PublishCustomActivities.md) -- When a user creates a custom activity and saves or submits it, that activity appears in the Custom and Packs tabs of the designer palette, but is only visible to the user who created it.
        - [Workflow activity pinning](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/workflow-activities/c_ActivityPinning.md) -- Workflow administrators can pin a custom activity to prevent the system from automatically updating that activity when a new version is downloaded from the ServiceNow Store.
  - [RaptorDB](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/raptordb.md) -- RaptorDB, the new ServiceNow database, optimizes ServiceNow AI Platform workloads, enabling your organization to support more users, workflows, and data on a single platform.
  - [ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-intro.md) -- ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner is a tool that enables you to leverage ServiceNow’s ability to intelligently generate regression tests for a specific instance.
    - [Exploring ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-landing.md) -- Use the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner application to generate tests depending on the instance behavior and workflows. You can also execute the generated tests using the Cloud Runner option.
    - [Configuring ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-configure.md) -- Configure the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application by setting up the cloud user.
      - [Set up cloud user](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-configure.md) -- Configure the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application by setting up the cloud user.
    - [Operating ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-operate.md) -- Operate the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application by generating tests automatically.
      - [Generating the tests](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-generate-tests.md) -- Generate tests automatically by leveraging ServiceNow Test Generation and Cloud Runner application. You can also run these tests with the click of a button.
      - [Tests generation for flow and flow logic](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-sc-flow.md) -- Use the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application to auto-generate tests that drive a ServiceNow flow for Service Catalog.
    - [Using ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-use.md) -- Use the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application to execute the generated tests using the Cloud Runner option. You can also schedule test execution and nightly runs to completely automate test execution.
      - [Executing the tests using Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-execute-tests.md) -- Execute the generated tests suite using the Cloud Runner browser option.
        - [Parallel testing using Cloud Runner](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-parallel-test.md) -- Parallel testing using the Cloud Runner involves execution of tests within a suite in parallel.
      - [Scheduling test execution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-schedule-test-run.md) -- Use schedules and nightly runs to execute tests and test suites in ServiceNow’s cloud infrastructure allowing you to fully automate ATF test executions.
    - [ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-ref.md) -- Reference information for the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner.
      - [Log entries in the BOQ form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-log-boq-form.md) -- Debug errors or track any critical transactions during the execution of the test suites, setting up the Cloud Runner or generating the tests by viewing the log entries in the BOQ form.
      - [ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-roles.md) -- ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner is installed with these roles.
        - [System Administrator [admin]](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/atf-test-generator-and-cloud-runner/atf-tg-cr-roles-1.md) -- The admin role is required to access and use the ATF Test Generator and Cloud Runner store application.
  - [HTTP Response Headers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/http-response-header.md) -- A response header is a simple name-value pair used in an HTTP response to provide additional information about page content or how the client should process it.
    - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/configure-http-response-header.md) -- Configure standard name-value pairs for HTTP response headers. You designate if the configuration applies to all pages, or to specific types (Service Portal, UI Page, or UX application record).
      - [Configuration for advanced users](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/configure-http-response-header.md) -- Configure standard name-value pairs for HTTP response headers. You designate if the configuration applies to all pages, or to specific types (Service Portal, UI Page, or UX application record).
  - [Response templates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/response-templates-templated-snippets.md) -- Resolve cases or support issues faster and more efficiently with response templates. Response templates (formerly known as templated snippets) are reusable messages that can be copied to case or task forms to provide quick and consistent messages to users, or to display standard chat response messages to requesters in Agent Chat.
    - [Activate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/activate-templated-snippets.md) -- You can activate response templates (formerly known as templated snippets) [com.sn_templated_snip] if you have the admin role. This plugin includes demo data and activates related plugins if they are not already active.
      - [Components installed with response templates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/components-installed-with-response-templates.md) -- Several types of components are installed with activation of the Templated Responses (com.sn_templated_snip) plugin, including tables and user roles.
    - [Create a response template](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/ai-platform-capabilities/create-modify-templated-snippet.md) -- Create or modify a response template (formerly known as a templated snippet) to define a reusable message that can be copied to case or task forms or used in Agent Chat sessions to provide quick and consistent messages to users.
  - [Policy as Code Engine (PaCE)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-managing-policies.md) -- You can administrate the full life cycle of policies using PaCE in applications by searching, reviewing, and updating existing policies, and creating policies as required. Policies in the applications are powered by PaCE.
    - [Understanding PaCE](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-understanding.md) -- PaCE enables you to manage, administer, and audit policies from a centralized location.
      - [Key user personas and roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-roles.md) -- This section describes different user personas and roles in PaCE. These personas are defined with the application where PaCE is being used.
      - [PaCE life cycle](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-lifecycle-management.md) -- This checklist lists the tasks involved in administering PaCE policies.
    - [Install PaCE](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-installation.md) -- Install PaCE.
    - [Administer PaCE policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-administer-policies.md) -- Use PaCE to manage the life cycle of a policy and create, update, review, and execute policies.
      - [Manage PaCE policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-admin-manage-policies.md) -- You can define and manage your PaCE policies in a single management console.
        - [View PaCE policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-view-existing-policies.md) -- You can view a list of all PaCE policies created by you and other policy administrators. The PaCE management console is your gateway to viewing and updating policies, and managing policy versions.
        - [Create PaCE policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-policy.md) -- Create a PaCE policy to determine if changes to a software service or application comply with a set of pre-defined rules.
        - [Activate or deactivate PaCE policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-deactivate-policy.md) -- You can activate or deactivate any PaCE policy. Only activated policies can validate object (tables and document IDs) changes. Deactivated policies are not executed, even if the policy is mapped. Deactivating a policy enables you to place the policy into a maintenance-like mode.
        - [Duplicate PaCE policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-duplicate.md) -- Create a duplicate of an existing policy.
        - [Edit PaCE policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-update-policy.md) -- You can update any existing PaCE policies, whether they are activated or deactivated.
        - [Delete](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-delete-policy.md) -- You can delete a PaCE policy at any time. All related versions for that policy are also deleted. However, for auditing reasons you cannot delete policies that were or are currently in use.
        - [Map PaCE policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-mapping-inputs.md) -- For a policy to be invoked correctly, it must be mapped to an object (table or document ID). The defined mapping settings are automatically verified when the policy is invoked.
          - [Define mapping inputs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-policy-mapping.md) -- You can map a policy to any number of objects. When you map a policy, validate the inputs, and execute the policy, a compliant, non-compliant, or compliant-exception decision is returned.
          - [Edit mapping inputs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-mapping-inputs.md) -- You can edit the mapping settings that map a policy to an object.
        - [PaCE mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-mapping.md) -- Static Mapping enables you to map a set of policies to a record or object such as an incident or deployable. Dynamic Mapping enables you to map a set of policies on a dynamic set of records or objects such as a set of incidents or a set of deployables.
          - [Map policies using Static Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-validate-static.md) -- Use Static Mapping to map multiple PaCE policies against a record such as a change request.
          - [Map policies using Dynamic Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-validate-dynamic.md) -- Use Dynamic Mapping to map multiple PaCE policies against specified conditions.
            - [Request exception](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-request-exception-dynamic-mapping.md) -- Request an exception on policies that are dynamically mapped to conditions.
        - [Create PaCE policy template](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-policy-template.md) -- Create PaCE templates for the PaCE policy forms that you use frequently.
      - [Manage PaCE policy versions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-versions.md) -- PaCE policy versions contain the actual policy script, as well as API Variable and Config Parameter definitions used for validating policies. Each policy can have many versions, each of which can be easily managed.
        - [PaCE version states](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-version-states.md) -- A PaCE policy version can be in one of four states: Draft, Current, Archived, and Now-Content. The policy version state also defines the version numbering.
        - [Create PaCE policy versions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-creating-policy-versions.md) -- You can create new versions for any of your existing policies.
          - [Create PaCE policy version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-policy-version.md) -- Create a version from scratch at any time for any of your existing PaCE policies.
          - [Create PaCE policy version in low-code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-policy-version-low-code.md) -- Create a policy version using low-code for any of your existing PaCE policies.
            - [Policy logic fields](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-logic-fields.md) -- Policy logic is a set of conditions that is used for determining whether a policy is compliant or non-compliant. You can use the condition builder to specify conditions for the policy.
          - [Create copy of an existing PaCE policy version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-copy-version.md) -- You can create a copy of an existing policy version. All the settings and mappings from the existing version are copied over to the new one.
          - [Duplicate PaCE policy versions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-version-duplicate_2.md) -- You can create a duplicate policy from a policy version or multiple versions from two or more policy versions. If you want to use a locked policy, you must first create a duplicate version and then modify the policy version as required.
          - [Edit PaCE policy script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-policy-script.md) -- Edit the PaCE policy script to apply any changes to the policy logic.
        - [Publish](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-publish-policy-version.md) -- You can publish a policy version at any time. When the policy is published, a compliant, non-compliant, or compliant-exception decision is made for objects (tables or document IDs) mapped to the policy.
        - [Edit draft policy version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-draft-version.md) -- You can edit a Draft PaCE policy version at any time. The Draft version can be published to become the Current version. You can also create multiple different Draft versions from different Archived versions.
        - [Edit archived PaCE policy version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-current-archive-version.md) -- To edit a Current (published) or Archived PaCE policy version, you must create a copy of that version, which can then be updated as required.
        - [Compare PaCE policy versions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-compare-versions.md) -- You can compare two versions of a PaCE policy, whatever their state. Any differences between the two selected versions are highlighted for quick analysis.
        - [Delete PaCE policy version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-delete-policy-version.md) -- You can delete a policy version at any time. However, only versions that have never been published can be deleted. Published versions are stored for auditing purposes.
      - [Passing parameters to PaCE policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-passing-parameters.md) -- Parameters can be passed to a PaCE policy to validate updates to an object (tables and document IDs). These variables apply to authoring in both low-code or JavaScript. Policy versions include three types of parameter inputs: API Variables, Config Parameters, and Record References.
        - [Create new variables for policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-variable-policy-version.md) -- Create an API Variable, Config Parameter, or Record Reference for the policy version in the Define Variables section of the Policy Builder.
          - [Data type descriptions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-data-type-descriptions.md) -- The following is a list of the most common data types for creating a variable.
        - [Create data collector](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-data-collector.md) -- Create a data collector for a policy version in the Define Variables section of the Policy Builder.
        - [Data collector structure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-data-collector-structure.md) -- This section describes the structure of a PaCE data collector.
        - [Activate and build data collectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-activate-data-collector.md) -- Activate and build data collectors to add to a policy.
        - [Test data collectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-test-data-collector.md) -- Test a data collector for a policy version in the Test Playground.
        - [Add data collectors](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-add-data-collectors.md) -- After testing the activated data collectors, add them to a policy in the Policy Builder.
        - [Add nested policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-add-nest-policies.md) -- Add existing policies to a policy's conditions to define the order a policy's execution and the conditions is executed.
      - [Use Test Playground](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-use-test-playground.md) -- The Test Playground enables you to test updates to a PaCE policy at any time, without the need to test or evaluate them in a Production environment.
        - [Test updates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-test-policy-version.md) -- Use the Test Playground to test updates to a PaCE policy script. You can evaluate changes to the policy script in real time, enabling you to determine if the changes that are applied render the policy compliant or non-compliant.
        - [Review output decision](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-review-output-decision.md) -- After evaluating any changes made to the PaCE policy script, you can review the execution output and compliance decision reached. You can review this output immediately after evaluating any policy script changes.
        - [Review execution log](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-review-execution-log.md) -- After evaluating any changes made to the PaCE policy script, you can review the execution log generated.
      - [Execute policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-execute-policies.md) -- Execute a policy to determine if the policy is compliant or non-compliant. Executions can be run as part of your policy evaluation process, or in real time when mapped to an object (table or document ID).I presume we should also mention invoking with the API? Do we want to tell the user how to do that exactly?
        - [Review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-review-executed-policies.md) -- You can review the execution activity of any policy at any time. Recorded execution activity includes the time taken to execute, decisions of compliance, execution types, and the outcome of the execution.
      - [Manage categories and tags](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-manage-categories.md) -- Use categories and tags to manage and group your PaCE policies more efficiently. As the policy administrator, you can create and edit any number of categories and tags.
        - [Manage PaCE categories](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-categories.md) -- Use the Categories option to group your PaCE policies more efficiently. As the policy administrator, you can create and edit any number of categories.
          - [Create](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-create-category.md) -- Create PaCE policy categories to assign to a policy when creating or updating a policy. Categories enable you to categorize and manage policies more efficiently.
          - [Edit or update](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-category.md) -- Update categories for a PaCE policy as required. You can also update categories for Active policies.
          - [View](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-view-categories.md) -- You can view the PaCE policies that have been assigned to a specific category. All the categories created, together with their associated policies, can be viewed in the All Categories table.
        - [Manage PaCE tags](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-tags.md) -- Use tags to manage your PaCE policies more efficiently. As the policy administrator, you can create and edit any number of tags.
          - [Add](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-add-tags-policies.md) -- Add or create policy tags on-the-fly for any PaCE policy, including currently Active policies. Tags enable you to manage policies more efficiently.
          - [Edit or remove](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-edit-tags.md) -- Edit and remove tags from a PaCE policy as required. When editing a tag, you can also define who can view the tag.
          - [View](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-view-tags.md) -- You can view the PaCE policies that have been assigned to a specific tag. All tags together with their associated policies can be viewed in the All Tags table.
    - [Write and test policies](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-write-test-policies.md) -- This section provides guidelines on how to write and test PaCE policies.
      - [Structure of a PaCE policy script](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-policy-structure.md) -- This section describes the structure of a PaCE policy script.
      - [Policy invocation API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-return-decisions.md) -- When a PaCE policy is invoked, the calling service passes the relevant object (tables and document IDs) that is being validated by providing the documentRecord object. A calling service is a service or product that uses the PaCE API. PaCE determines which policies must be executed by using the mapping table and the documentRecord.
        - [Sample PaCE API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-inv-api.md) -- The following is a sample invocation request when the PaCE API is executed.
      - [PaCE API invocation parameters](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-invocation-params.md) -- This table describes the properties that can be configured when the PaCE API is invoked. These properties can be set whenever the API is invoked or when you test a policy in the Test Playground.
    - [Policy exceptions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-exception-about.md) -- By integrating Policy as Code Engine with Integrated Risk Management and Policy and Compliance Management workspace, the Compliance administrator can manage an organization's overall compliance posture and provide a business and regulatory context for codified policies.
      - [Enable](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-exception-enable.md) -- Enable exceptions for a PaCE policy and associate it with a control objective in GRC.
      - [Disable](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-exception-disable.md) -- You can disable exceptions for a policy for which exceptions have been granted. When this option is selected, you can no longer request exceptions and control objectives can no longer be associated with the policy.
      - [Request](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/policy-createmap-exception.md) -- After a policy has been enabled and configured for exceptions, you can map a policy to a deployable and request an exception.
      - [Withdraw](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-withdraw-exception.md) -- If an exception is no longer required, you can withdraw a granted exception request for a policy.
    - [Integrate PaCE with other applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/policy-as-code-engine-pace/pace-integrate-other-apps.md) -- Extend the capabilities of Policy as Code Engine (PaCE) by creating policies with other applications.
- [Password Reset and Password Change reports and logs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/password-reset/c_MonitorPasswordResetActivity.md) -- The Password Reset application provides several tools for monitoring and troubleshooting password reset activities.
- [Password Reset and Password Change reports and logs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/password-reset/c_MonitorPasswordResetActivity.md) -- The Password Reset application provides several tools for monitoring and troubleshooting password reset activities.
- [Unblock a Password Reset user](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/password-reset/t_UnblockAUser.md) -- If a user is manually banned or is flagged as exceeding max attempts, the user can be blocked (not allowed to use the Password Reset application). You can unblock a blocked user.
- [Password Reset and Password Change reports and logs](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/servicenow-platform/password-reset/c_MonitorPasswordResetActivity.md) -- The Password Reset application provides several tools for monitoring and troubleshooting password reset activities.
